They correctly state that the code is boilerplate and safe.\n\n---\n\n### Critical Evaluation & Recommendations:\n- The code is **not obfuscated** (scores 0), which aligns with the source mapping URL.\n- The **malware score (0)** is justified; no malicious behavior is present.\n- The **security risk (0.1)** is a conservative estimate acknowledging that misuse of the contract connection could pose some risk, but this is inherent to any contract interaction, not a flaw in the code.\n\n### Final assessment:\n- The **malware score** should remain **0**.\n- The **obfuscated score** should stay **0**.\n- The **risk score** can stay at **0.1** or be slightly lowered to **0**, given the code's benign nature, but 0.1 is acceptable as a conservative estimate.\n\n---\n\n### My Overall Estimated Scores:\n- **Malware:** 0  \n- **Obfuscated:** 0  \n- **Risk:** 0.1 (or 0 for absolute safety, but 0.1 is reasonable considering the context)  \n\n**Conclusion:**  \nAll reports are accurate in their assessment.
